nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: 非法猎捕
nodes.summary: 直接捕杀或非法贸易导致种群下降。

nodes.name: 印度尼西亚
nodes.summary: 印度尼西亚是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联297种鸟类，包括细斑树鸭、白腹麻鸭、太平洋黑鴨等297种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 龙目岛
nodes.summary: 龙目岛是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联8种鸟类，包括斑姬地鸠、Javan plover、白面鷺等8种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 松巴哇島
nodes.summary: 松巴哇島是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联8种鸟类，包括斑姬地鸠、Javan plover、白面鷺等8种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 热带雨林
nodes.summary: 结构复杂且冠层完整的高生物多样性森林。

nodes.name: Flores hawk-eagle
nodes.summary: Flores hawk-eagle（Flores hawk-eagle，学名 Nisaetus floris），在图谱中标记为濒危（EN）物种，已关联6个分布地点节点，常见栖息地包括热带雨林，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏、非法猎捕。

nodes.name: EN
nodes.summary: EN表示 IUCN 濒危等级，当前关联384种鸟类，如朱鹮、中华秋沙鸭、黑脸琵鹭等384种鸟类。

nodes.name: 小巽他群岛
nodes.summary: 小巽他群岛是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联35种鸟类，包括橙脚冢雉、蓝胸鹑、Pink-headed imperial pigeon等35种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 弗洛勒斯岛
nodes.summary: 弗洛勒斯岛是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联7种鸟类，包括Javan plover、白面鷺、Flores hawk-eagle等7种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 热带雨林
nodes.summary: 热带雨林是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联12种鸟类，包括Silky-tailed nightjar、黑顶蛙口鸱、黑水鸡等12种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: It mainly occurs in lowlands, but has been recorded at altitudes as high as . The Flores hawk-eagle is known to be reliant on tropical rainforest. Most frequently they are seen flying over the canopy over slight slopes of the forest. They are sometimes reported as foraging over cultivations but this is only in areas adjacent to large stretches of forest.{{cite book|author=Coates, B.J

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: |year=2004 |url=http://www.orientalbirdclub.org/wp-content/uploads/2012/09/Gjershaug-Flores.pdf |title=The taxonomic status of Flores Hawk-Eagle Spizaetus floris |journal=Forktail |volume=20|pages= 55–62}} A forest dwelling predator, this species is classified as endangered due to habitat loss and persecution.{{cite journal|author=Raharjaningtrah, W

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: Due to ongoing habitat loss within its small range, capture for the cage bird trade and persecution due to its habit of taking chickens, the Flores hawk-eagle is evaluated as endangered on the IUCN Red List of Threatened Species. It is probably the world's rarest eagle species. Poaching may be ongoing and trappers may even come from other islands to kill these birds. However, the Flores hawk-eagle was once considered in western Flores be a "totem" or "empo," i.e., a human ancestor, and was not persecuted or killed but habitat destruction may have been long negatively effecting the species. Educational efforts have been undertaken to try to ensure local people's coexistence with this predatory bird. It is estimated that less than 100 pairs remain, based on a per pair occupancy of about . A more specific estimate put the total at about 75 projected pairs, with 10 pairs on Lombok, 38 pairs on Sumbawa, and 27 pairs on Flores
